text: The surgeon forms the Roux limb by dividing the jejunum 30 centimeters from the ligamentum teres. A surgical stapler is used to transect the mesentery, allowing the Roux limb to be mobilized towards the stomach.

The surgeon initiates a side-to-side anastomosis 90 centimeters from the cut end of the Roux limb. An endolinear stapler is used to create the anastomosis, forming an opening with the retrocolic Roux limb for the gastrojejunostomy.

The surgeon initiates the formation of the gastric pouch by creating a horizontal row using an endogenous stapler, followed by a vertical row with a blue cartridge. Circular stapler and O-reveal are not utilized in this step.
The surgeon begins the end-to-side anastomosis using an endogealinear stapler, fixing the Roux limb to the stomach. The gastrointestinal opening is then closed with sutures.
Thank you very much for watching just a brief demonstration. of Roux-en-Y gastric bypass.
